summary:
The query asks for exceptions among actual clinical trial phases—I.e., those conducted directly in humans—which exclude preclinical testing. Among these, while early-phase trials adhere closely to rigorous GCP standards including randomization and blinded designs, Phase IV introduces significant deviations through its reliance on passive data collection methods like spontaneous reports instead of structured interventions or comparisons. Because Phase IV lacks formalized study designs typical of other stages—including predefined outcomes, interventional components—and depends more on observation than experimental manipulation, this represents the most notable departure from classic GCP tenets typically associated with high-quality, actively managed trials during product development.

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{D}}$  
(Note again: Despite stating ‘Phase V’, clearly refers to what should have been labeled 'Phase IV'—a known typographic/labeling issue.)
